title: ISSS608-VAA
text: Welcome to ISSS608 Visual Analytics and Applications. In this website, you will find my coursework prepared for this course.

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
The code chunk below uses p_load() of pacman package to check if tidyverse packages are installed in the computer. If they are, then they will be launched into R.

pacman::p_load(tidyverse)





exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

Rows: 322 Columns: 7
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (4): ID, CLASS, GENDER, RACE
dbl (3): ENGLISH, MATHS, SCIENCE

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.


#Essential Grammatical Elements in ggplot2:geom

















Bin width defaults to 1/30 of the range of the data. Pick better value with
`binwidth`.










ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  
  geom_dotplot(binwidth=2.5,
               dotsize= 0.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(NULL,
                      breaks = NULL)












ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram()

`stat_bin()` using `bins = 30`. Pick better value `binwidth`.










ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20,
                 color = "black",
                 fill = "lightblue")











ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           fill = GENDER)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20,
                color = "gray")













ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_density()









ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           colours = GENDER)) +
  geom_density()












ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot()











ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) + 
  geom_boxplot(notch = TRUE)













ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_violin()












ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           y = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point()












ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(position = "jitter",
             size = 0.5)

text:
The code chunk below uses p_load() of pacman package to check if tidyverse packages are installed in the computer. If they are, then they will be launched into R.

pacman::p_load(tidyverse)

text:

  geom_violin()

       aes(x = MATHS,

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
stat_summary() method

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  stat_summary(geom = "point",
               fun = "mean",
               colour = "red",
               size = 4)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
geom() method

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(y = MATHS,
           x = GENDER)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  geom_point(stat = "summary",
             fun = "mean",
             colour = "red",
             size = 4)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
section: Addming Best Fit Curve for SCatter plot
text:
Addming Best Fit Curve for SCatter plot

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           y = ENGLISH)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(size = 0.5)

Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.


`geom_smooth()` using method = 'loess' and formula = 'y ~ x'










ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           y = ENGLISH)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = lm,
              linewidth = 0.5)

`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
facet_grid()

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS)) + 
  geom_histogram(bins = 20) +
  facet_grid(~ CLASS)

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
coord_flip()

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = RACE)) +
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip()









fixing x and y range using coord_cartesian()

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = MATHS,
           y = ENGLISH)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = lm,
              size = 0.5) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0,100),
                  ylim = c(0,100))

`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
theme_gray()

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = RACE)) + 
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_gray()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
theme_classic()

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = RACE)) + 
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_classic()

title: Hands-on Exercise 1
text:
Theme Minimal

ggplot(data = exam_data,
       aes(x = RACE)) + 
  geom_bar() +
  coord_flip() +
  theme_minimal()

title: About
text:
About this site

title: Hands-on Exercise 2
text:
pacman::p_load(ggrepel, patchwork, 
               ggthemes, hrbrthemes,
               tidyverse) 





exam_data <- read_csv("data/Exam_data.csv")

Rows: 322 Columns: 7
── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Delimiter: ","
chr (4): ID, CLASS, GENDER, RACE
dbl (3): ENGLISH, MATHS, SCIENCE

ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.


#Essential Grammatical Elements in ggplot2:geom





Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.


`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'














`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'


Warning: ggrepel: 317 unlabeled data points (too many overlaps). Consider
increasing max.overlaps













ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  theme_gray() +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") 











ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_economist()













ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )









ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 18),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )












p1 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = MATHS)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") + 
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of Maths scores")
p1









p2 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x = ENGLISH)) +
  geom_histogram(bins=20, 
                 boundary = 100,
                 color="grey25", 
                 fill="grey90") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("Distribution of English scores")
p2









  p3 <- ggplot(data=exam_data, 
             aes(x= MATHS, 
                 y=ENGLISH)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method=lm, 
              size=0.5) +  
  coord_cartesian(xlim=c(0,100),
                  ylim=c(0,100)) +
  ggtitle("English scores versus Maths scores for Primary 3")
p3

`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'












p1 + p2













(p1 / p2) | p3

`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'













((p1 / p2) | p3) + 
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'I')

`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'













p3 + inset_element(p2, 
                   left = 0.02, 
                   bottom = 0.7, 
                   right = 0.5, 
                   top = 1)

`geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
